engineGuide.h2: Dodge Charger Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Dodge Charger across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: Post-2011 Eagle HEMI with VVT and MDS; the most common modern Charger V8 replacement.
compatibleVariants: R/T, R/T Plus, Pursuit Police Package, SXT V8 (select markets)
commonFailures: MDS lifter collapse causing camshaft scoring (60k-100k miles typical)
Exhaust manifold bolt fracture leading to exhaust leaks
Increased oil consumption from worn piston rings
entries.cta: Get quotes for EZH Dodge Charger engine replacement

entries.title: 5.7L Petrol
entries.history: Pre-Eagle non-VVT 5.7 HEMI; robust but lacks modern fuel economy features.
commonFailures: Lifter tick from worn hydraulic lifters
Oil pump pressure drop at idle
Valve seat recession on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for ESG Dodge Charger engine replacement

entries.title: 6.4L Petrol
entries.history: Apache-coded 392 HEMI; high-output naturally aspirated V8 for Scat Pack and SRT models.
compatibleVariants: Scat Pack, SRT 392, Daytona 392, Widebody variants
commonFailures: Piston ring land failure under aggressive tuning
Oil pump gear wear reducing idle pressure
Valve train component fatigue from high-RPM use
entries.cta: Get quotes for BGE Dodge Charger engine replacement

entries.title: 3.6L Petrol
entries.history: Chrysler's high-volume Pentastar V6; economical entry point for modern Charger buyers.
compatibleVariants: SE, SXT, GT AWD, Base fleet models
commonFailures: Left cylinder head rocker arm tick (casting issue)
Oil filter housing leak from plastic canister failure
Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattling noise

entries.title: 7.2L Petrol
entries.history: Legendary RB-series big block; high-demand among classic Charger restorers in the UK.
commonFailures: Overheating from original cooling system limitations
Oil gallery cracking on high-mileage castings
Carburetor fuel delivery issues on unrestored units

commonProblems.h2: Dodge Charger Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Dodge Charger's HEMI V8 and Pentastar V6 engines are powerful but have documented failure patterns that drive UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address original failure points - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: HEMI Lifter Tick & MDS Failure - Why 5.7/6.4 Charger Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Charger R/T, Scat Pack, SRT 392 (2006-2023, EZH/ESG/BGE HEMI codes)
problems.rootCause: MDS (Multi-Displacement System) lifters collapse due to oil pressure loss or wear, causing camshaft lobes to grind and metal debris to circulate.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all 16 lifters, inspect camshaft, flush oil system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before cam damage; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace camshaft, all lifters, timing components, full gasket set
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses damage after lifter collapse; engine remains original spec

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with upgraded lifters, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage or severely damaged engines; eliminates recurring issues

problems.recommendation: If you hear a persistent tick at idle on a 5.7 or 6.4 HEMI Charger, get it diagnosed immediately. On a Charger worth £15k-£35k, a rebuilt engine with upgraded MDS components often offers better long-term value than repeated cam/lifter repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EZH/BGE HEMI engine prices for your Dodge Charger

problems.h4: Pentastar V6 Rocker Arm Tick - Common on 3.6L Charger Engines
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Charger SE, SXT, GT AWD (2011-2023, ERB/ERC Pentastar codes)
problems.rootCause: Casting inconsistency in left cylinder head causes rocker arm pivot wear, creating metallic tapping noise and potential valve damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace affected rocker arms, adjust valve clearance, refresh oil
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if head casting is sound; monitor for recurrence

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, machine and refit left cylinder head with new valves/seats
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause on one bank; right bank may follow later

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt short/long block with updated head castings, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution for high-mileage V6 Chargers; eliminates repeat failures

problems.recommendation: Pentastar rocker tick is often an early warning. On a Charger SE/SXT valued under £12k, a rebuilt engine with updated components typically costs less than repeated head repairs and restores full reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for ERB Pentastar engine replacement for your Dodge Charger

problems.h4: Exhaust Manifold Bolt Failure - HEMI Leak & Rattle
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Charger R/T, Scat Pack (2006-2023, all HEMI codes)
problems.rootCause: Thermal cycling causes factory exhaust manifold bolts to fatigue and snap, creating exhaust leaks, rattles, and potential oxygen sensor errors.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drill/extract broken bolts, install stainless steel replacements, new gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Cost-effective if no manifold warpage; prevents recurrence with upgraded hardware

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply and fit new/reconditioned exhaust manifolds with upgraded bolts
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if original manifolds are cracked or warped from heat stress

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Not typically a standalone replacement trigger
repairOptions.longevity: Usually addressed during other engine work; not a primary failure mode

problems.recommendation: Exhaust bolt failure is annoying but rarely catastrophic. Address it early with stainless hardware to avoid costly extraction later. Only consider engine replacement if bolt failure coincides with other major HEMI issues.
problems.cta: Find HEMI exhaust repair specialists for your Dodge Charger

problems.h4: 2.7L V6 Sludge Failure - Early LX Chargers (2006-2010)
problems.affectedModels: Dodge Charger Base, SE (2006-2010, 2.7L V6 code)
problems.rootCause: Inadequate oil flow design and extended service intervals cause sludge buildup, starving bearings and leading to complete engine seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Chemical flush, oil pan removal, screen cleaning, fresh oil/filter
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable as preventative; ineffective once bearings are damaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace crank, rods, pistons; reuse heads if undamaged
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if cylinder heads are sound; addresses core sludge damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 2.7L or swap to 3.6L Pentastar (if compatible)
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable path; many owners opt for Pentastar upgrade during replacement

problems.recommendation: The 2.7L is prone to fatal sludge failure. If your early LX Charger has this engine and shows oil pressure warnings, replacement is usually more economical than repeated repairs. Consider a Pentastar swap for long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare 2.7L and 3.6L engine options for your early Dodge Charger

problems.h4: Oil Pressure Loss at Idle - HEMI Bearing Risk
problems.rootCause: Oil pump gear wear or pickup tube screen clogging reduces pressure at idle, risking main/rod bearing wear and eventual engine knock.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil pump, clean pickup screen, flush galleries, fresh oil
repairOptions.longevity: Preventative if caught early; restores pressure before bearing damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove oil pan, replace main/rod bearings, inspect crank, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses early bearing wear; requires precise machining and assembly

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt HEMI with Upgraded Pump
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt long block with high-flow oil pump, upgraded bearings, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage engines; eliminates recurring oil pressure concerns

problems.recommendation: Low oil pressure at idle on a HEMI Charger is a serious warning. If bearings are already scored, a rebuilt engine with upgraded oiling components offers better value than repeated bearing jobs.
problems.cta: Get quotes for HEMI oil system rebuilt engines for your Dodge Charger

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Dodge Charger
finalCta.paragraph: From HEMI lifter tick to Pentastar rocker failure, the Dodge Charger's most common engine problems have proven solutions. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing these exact failure points. Compare prices, check warranty terms, and arrange nationwide supply & fit - all without the main dealer premium. Get your tailored quote today and keep your Charger on the road with confidence.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Dodge Charger Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Dodge Charger and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Dodge Charger Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Dodge Charger engine type is available through our UK network, with typical price ranges based on variant, HEMI/Pentastar code and budget.

types.title: Dodge Charger Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Professionally inspected, cleaned and tested used HEMI or Pentastar engines with warranty coverage.
types.frontDescription: Professionally inspected, cleaned and tested used HEMI or Pentastar engines with warranty coverage.
types.frontDisclaimer: Mileage and condition vary; warranty terms differ by supplier.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned units are inspected, compression-tested engines sourced from UK breakers or US imports. Ideal for budget-conscious Charger owners needing a reliable replacement fast.
backBullets: Typically 60k-120k miles, full service history preferred
3-6 month warranty standard; 12-month available from select suppliers
Fast turnaround: 3-7 days UK stock, 2-4 weeks US import
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Dodge Charger engine prices

types.title: Dodge Charger Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Disassembled, machined and rebuilt HEMI/Pentastar engines with upgraded wear components and full warranty.
types.frontDescription: Disassembled, machined and rebuilt HEMI/Pentastar engines with upgraded wear components and full warranty.
types.frontDisclaimer: Price reflects engine code, mileage and upgrade level.
types.backDescription: Rebuilt engines undergo full machining, new bearings/seals/gaskets, and upgraded parts addressing known Charger failure points like MDS lifters or Pentastar rockers.
backBullets: Zero-mile on rebuilt components; bench-tested before dispatch
Best value for high-mileage or failed engines needing long-term reliability
types.cta: Get rebuilt Dodge Charger engine quotes

types.title: Dodge Charger Used Engines
types.description: Second-hand HEMI or V6 engines from accident-damaged Chargers, tested and ready for installation.
types.frontDescription: Second-hand HEMI or V6 engines from accident-damaged Chargers, tested and ready for installation.
types.frontDisclaimer: Mileage unverified; limited or no warranty on some units.
types.backDescription: Used engines offer the lowest upfront cost for Charger repairs. Best for short-term fixes, project cars, or when budget is the primary constraint.
backBullets: Mileage varies widely; request compression test results
30-90 day warranty typical; inspect thoroughly before purchase
Ideal for older Chargers or temporary solutions pending upgrade
types.cta: Check used Dodge Charger engine availability

types.title: Dodge Charger Remanufactured Engines
types.description: Factory-spec remanufactured HEMI/Pentastar engines built to original tolerances with OEM-grade components.
types.frontDescription: Factory-spec remanufactured HEMI/Pentastar engines built to original tolerances with OEM-grade components.
types.frontDisclaimer: Premium pricing reflects OEM-spec machining and components.
types.backDescription: Remanufactured engines are rebuilt to factory specifications using OEM or equivalent parts. Ideal for collectors, warranty-sensitive owners, or maximum longevity.
backBullets: CNC-machined blocks, OEM-spec bearings, seals and gaskets
24-36 month warranty available from specialist suppliers
Best for classic Chargers or owners planning long-term retention
types.cta: Compare remanufactured Dodge Charger engine costs

types.title: Dodge Charger Refurbished Engines
types.description: Partially reconditioned engines with key wear items replaced, balancing cost and reliability for Charger repairs.
types.frontDescription: Partially reconditioned engines with key wear items replaced, balancing cost and reliability for Charger repairs.
types.frontDisclaimer: Scope of refurbishment varies; confirm included components.
types.backDescription: Refurbished engines address specific failure points (e.g., lifters, rockers) without full rebuild. Cost-effective for targeted repairs on otherwise sound Charger engines.
backBullets: Focus on known weak areas: MDS lifters, oil pumps, timing components
Suitable for Chargers with isolated failures and good overall condition
types.cta: View refurbished Dodge Charger engine options

types.title: Dodge Charger Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Complete engine replacement service including supply, fitting, fluids and testing at vetted UK specialist garages.
types.frontDescription: Complete engine replacement service including supply, fitting, fluids and testing at vetted UK specialist garages.
types.frontDisclaimer: Labour cost varies by location, engine complexity and garage rates.
types.backDescription: Supply & fit bundles engine supply with professional installation at UK specialists familiar with American V8s and Charger electronics. Hassle-free, warranty-backed service.
backBullets: Includes removal, fitting, ECU relearn, road test and handover
Labour rates £80-£150/hour; HEMI swaps take 12-20 hours typically
Ideal for owners without specialist tools or HEMI/Pentastar experience

440 RB/383 B - Classic big block V8s (1966-1974), restoration demand

engineYears.h2: Dodge Charger Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Dodge Charger's production timeline, organised so UK owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The modern Charger returned on the LX platform, offering V6 and 5.7L HEMI V8 options for the UK import market.
keyChanges: Modern Charger revived on rear-wheel-drive LX platform shared with Chrysler 300
Base 2.7L and 3.5L V6 engines offered for fleet and entry models
knownFor: ESG HEMI lifter tick emerging as high-mileage issue
2.7L V6 prone to oil sludge if service intervals extended
Strong US domestic demand limiting UK import availability early on
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2010 Dodge Charger replacement engines
years.ticker: Dodge Charger 5.7 HEMI replacement • 2.7L V6 sludge fix • LX Charger engine cost

years.description: The LD update introduced the Eagle 5.7L HEMI with VVT and refined Pentastar V6, improving efficiency and performance for UK buyers.
keyChanges: Eagle-coded 5.7L HEMI (EZH) added VVT and improved MDS reliability
3.6L Pentastar V6 (ERB/ERC) replaced older V6 units across the range
8-speed automatic transmission introduced for better fuel economy
mainEngines: ERB/ERC - 3.6L Pentastar V6 (292-300 hp) (SE, SXT, GT)
knownFor: EZH HEMI lifter tick still occurs but with improved diagnostics
Pentastar V6 rocker arm tick on left cylinder head becomes common failure
Improved UK parts availability as LD generation volumes increased
years.cta: Compare 2011-2014 Dodge Charger engine replacement costs
years.ticker: Charger EZH lifter replacement • Pentastar rocker tick fix • LD Charger engine price

years.description: Performance variants exploded with Scat Pack, SRT 392 and Hellcat models, bringing Apache 6.4L and supercharged 6.2L HEMI engines to UK enthusiasts.
keyChanges: 6.4L Apache HEMI (BGE) introduced for Scat Pack and SRT 392 models
Supercharged 6.2L Hellcat HEMI launched, offering 707-797hp factory output
Widebody packages and special editions increased collector interest in UK
mainEngines: BGE - 6.4L Apache HEMI (475-485 hp) (Scat Pack, SRT 392)
EZH - 5.7L HEMI VVT (continued in R/T, Pursuit)
knownFor: Apache HEMI piston ring land sensitivity under aggressive tuning
Hellcat supercharger coupler wear and intercooler heat soak concerns
High UK demand for performance variants driving premium replacement pricing
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2023 Dodge Charger performance engines
years.ticker: Scat Pack 6.4L rebuild • Hellcat supercharger service • SRT 392 engine cost UK

years.description: The original muscle car era Chargers featured legendary Mopar big block and small block V8s, now highly sought after by UK restorers.
keyChanges: Iconic B-body platform with hidden headlights and Coke-bottle styling
Engine range spanned from 318 LA small block to 440 RB and 426 HEMI big blocks
UK import market now drives demand for date-code correct replacement engines

faq.h2: Dodge Charger Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Dodge Charger HEMI engine in the UK?
items.answer: Replacing a 5.7L or 6.4L HEMI engine in a Dodge Charger typically costs £3,200-£12,500 for a rebuilt unit (supply only), depending on engine code, mileage and upgrade level. Labour for supply & fit adds £1,200-£2,800 at UK specialists.
keyPoints: 5.7L HEMI rebuilt: £3,200-£7,500 supply only
6.4L Apache HEMI rebuilt: £5,800-£12,500 supply only
Supply & fit labour typically adds £1,200-£2,800
Prices vary by engine code, mileage and warranty terms
items.cta: Compare rebuilt HEMI engine prices for your Dodge Charger

items.question: What causes the HEMI tick in Dodge Charger engines?
items.answer: The infamous HEMI tick is usually caused by MDS (Multi-Displacement System) lifter collapse, which can score the camshaft and circulate metal debris. It typically appears between 60k-100k miles on 5.7L and 6.4L HEMI Chargers.
keyPoints: MDS lifters lose oil pressure and collapse at idle
Collapsed lifters grind camshaft lobes, causing metallic ticking
Ignoring the tick risks complete engine failure from debris circulation
items.cta: Get quotes for HEMI lifter repair or engine replacement

items.question: Is the 3.6L Pentastar V6 in the Dodge Charger reliable?
items.answer: The 3.6L Pentastar V6 is generally reliable but has a documented left-cylinder-head rocker arm tick issue affecting many UK Chargers between 40k-70k miles. Early diagnosis and repair prevent costly valve damage.
keyPoints: Rocker arm tick stems from casting inconsistency in left cylinder head
Oil filter housing leaks and timing chain wear are secondary concerns
Rebuilt engines with updated head castings eliminate repeat failures
items.cta: Compare Pentastar V6 engine replacement options for your Charger

items.question: Can I swap a 6.4L HEMI into my 5.7L Dodge Charger?
items.answer: Yes, a 6.4L Apache HEMI can be swapped into a 5.7L Charger, but it requires PCM reprogramming, wiring harness modifications, and potentially upgraded cooling and exhaust systems. Budget £8k-£15k total for a professional conversion.
keyPoints: Engine mounts and transmission are compatible on LX/LD platforms
PCM and wiring harness must be updated for 6.4L calibration
Upgraded cooling, exhaust and fuel delivery recommended for reliability
items.cta: Enquire about 5.7L to 6.4L HEMI conversion for your Dodge Charger

items.question: How long should a Dodge Charger HEMI engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, a Dodge Charger HEMI engine can last 150,000-200,000 miles. However, MDS-related lifter failures commonly occur between 60k-100k miles if oil changes are delayed or low-quality oil is used.
keyPoints: Full synthetic oil changes every 5,000 miles extend HEMI life significantly
Avoid extended idling and short trips that prevent oil warming
Address lifter tick immediately to prevent camshaft and bearing damage
items.cta: Find HEMI maintenance specialists for your Dodge Charger

items.question: Do I need to notify the DVLA after replacing my Dodge Charger engine?
items.answer: Yes, UK law requires you to notify the DVLA of an engine number change on your V5C logbook. This is critical for insurance validity, MOT compliance, and future resale of your imported Dodge Charger.
keyPoints: Submit V62 form or update online via DVLA website with new engine number
Keep receipts and paperwork from the engine supplier and fitter
Failure to update can invalidate insurance and complicate MOT testing
items.cta: Get quotes for DVLA-compliant engine replacement for your Dodge Charger

items.question: Where can I find Dodge Charger engines in UK stock?
items.answer: UK stock for Dodge Charger engines is limited due to low import volumes. EngineMarket connects you with specialist breakers and rebuilders who hold UK inventory or can source from the US with transparent shipping timelines.
keyPoints: Most UK specialists hold Pentastar V6 and 5.7L HEMI units
6.4L and Hellcat engines often require US sourcing (2-4 week lead time)
Always confirm warranty terms and return policies before committing
items.cta: Check UK stock availability for your Dodge Charger engine

items.question: What's the difference between reconditioned, rebuilt and remanufactured Charger engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines are inspected used units; rebuilt engines are disassembled, machined and fitted with new wear components; remanufactured engines are built to factory specs with OEM-grade parts. Rebuilt offers the best balance of cost and reliability for most UK Charger owners.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: lowest cost, variable mileage, shorter warranty
Rebuilt: upgraded components, 12-month warranty, best value
Remanufactured: OEM-spec, premium pricing, longest warranty
items.cta: Compare engine condition options for your Dodge Charger replacement

items.question: Will a Dodge Challenger engine fit my Charger?
items.answer: Yes, engines from the Dodge Challenger (LX/LD platform) are directly compatible with the Dodge Charger, as both share the same chassis, wiring architecture and ECU programming. This expands your sourcing options for replacement engines in the UK.
keyPoints: 3.6L Pentastar, 5.7L HEMI and 6.4L Apache swap directly between models
Hellcat supercharged engines require matching PCM and calibration files
Always verify engine code and model year compatibility before purchasing
items.cta: Find compatible Challenger/Charger engines for your replacement

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in an older Dodge Charger?
items.answer: Replacing the engine in a Charger valued under £15k is often worthwhile if the chassis and interior are sound, as a rebuilt engine with warranty can extend the car's life by 50k-100k miles. For classic Chargers, correct-engine replacements preserve collector value.
keyPoints: Compare replacement cost against current market value of your Charger
Rebuilt engines with warranty offer better long-term value than repeated repairs
Classic Chargers with correct engines command premium resale prices in the UK
items.cta: Get a tailored quote to assess engine replacement value for your Dodge Charger
